skill.name: python-executor
skill.description: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via [inference.sh](https://inference.sh). Pre-installed: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, Playwright, MoviePy, Pillow, OpenCV, trimesh, and 100+ more libraries. Use for: data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, video creation, 3D model processing, PDF generation, API calls, automation scripts. Triggers: python, execute code, run script, web scraping, data analysis, image processing, video editing, 3D models, automation, pandas, matplotlib
skill.summary: Run Python code remotely in a sandbox with 100+ pre-installed libraries via the belt CLI.
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation-only skill that describes how to invoke the inference.sh Python execution service via the belt CLI. The static analyzer flagged 70 patterns, but all are false positives: 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are Markdown formatting for code spans (e.g., `belt login`), 'Python HTTP libraries' are example code shown in documentation, 'Hardcoded URL' findings are legitimate service URLs and example endpoints, and the 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'Network reconnaissance' findings are triggered by the words 'safe' and 'isolated subprocess' with no actual crypto or network recon code present. The allowed-tools is narrowly scoped to Bash(belt *) and code execution occurs remotely in inference.sh's sandbox, not locally. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: External network access required for service invocation
low_findings.description: The skill invokes the inference.sh Python execution service, which requires network connectivity. All code execution occurs in inference.sh's remote sandbox; the local machine only sends code to the API and receives results. This is the intended and documented behavior of the skill.

low_findings.title: Bash command execution via belt CLI
low_findings.description: The skill uses Bash(belt *) to invoke the belt CLI, which calls the inference.sh service. The allowed-tools is narrowly scoped to belt commands only, preventing arbitrary shell execution. This is the documented mechanism for using the skill.

content.user_title: Execute Python Code in a Sandboxed Environment
content.value_statement: Setting up Python environments with specialized libraries takes time and effort. This skill runs Python code in a remote sandbox with 100+ libraries pre-installed, accessible via a simple CLI command.
actual_capabilities: Run Python code in a remote sandboxed environment via belt CLI
Use 100+ pre-installed libraries including NumPy, Pandas, and Matplotlib
Scrape websites with requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, and Playwright
Process and analyze data with visualization and export to files
Create images, videos, and 3D models with Pillow, MoviePy, and trimesh
Generate PDFs and call external APIs from within the sandbox
limitations: CPU-only environment with no GPU or ML acceleration support
Maximum execution timeout of 300 seconds per script
Requires belt CLI installation and an inference.sh account
Cannot install additional packages beyond the pre-installed library set

use_cases.title: Web scraping and data extraction
use_cases.description: Extract data from websites using BeautifulSoup or Selenium, then save results to files. Ideal for research and data collection tasks.

use_cases.title: Data analysis with visualization
use_cases.description: Process datasets with Pandas and create charts with Matplotlib or Plotly. Output files are automatically returned from the sandbox.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts and business intelligence professionals

use_cases.title: Media processing and generation
use_cases.description: Manipulate images with PIL, create videos with MoviePy, or process 3D models with trimesh. All output files are captured automatically.

prompt_templates.title: Run a simple Python script
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this Python code in the sandbox: print('Hello, World!')
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner testing the basic execution flow

prompt_templates.title: Scrape a webpage and extract the title
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the python-executor to scrape https://example.com and print the page title using requests and BeautifulSoup.

prompt_templates.title: Create a bar chart from data
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the python-executor to create a bar chart showing sales data: Alice had 100 sales, Bob had 150. Save the chart to outputs/chart.png.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a video with text overlay
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the python-executor to create a 3-second video with a blue background and white text saying 'Hello!'. Use MoviePy and save to outputs/hello.mp4 with a 120-second timeout.

output_examples.input: Run print('Hello, World!') in the sandbox
Generate a bar chart from sales data and save to outputs/
output_examples.output: Chart saved! Output file: outputs/chart.png (PNG image, 480x360 pixels)

output_examples.input: Scrape https://api.github.com/users/octocat and print the JSON response
output_examples.output: JSON response with user profile data including login, id, avatar_url, and public_repos fields, formatted with indentation

best_practices: Save output files to the outputs/ directory for automatic return from the sandbox
Use plt.savefig() instead of plt.show() because the sandbox is non-interactive
Use the high_memory variant (16GB RAM) for large datasets that exceed the default 8GB limit
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to install packages - only the 100+ pre-installed libraries are available
Avoid long-running scripts that exceed the 300-second timeout limit
Do not use plt.show() or other interactive display functions - the sandbox cannot display them

faq.question: What Python version is used in the sandbox?
faq.answer: Python 3.10 in a CPU-only environment with 8GB RAM by default.

faq.question: How much memory is available?
faq.answer: 8GB by default. Use the high_memory variant for 16GB of RAM with large datasets.

faq.question: Can I install additional Python packages?
faq.answer: No. The sandbox includes 100+ pre-installed libraries like NumPy, Pandas, and Matplotlib, but you cannot add new packages.

faq.question: How are output files returned to me?
faq.answer: Files saved to the outputs/ directory are automatically detected and returned in the response.

faq.question: Is there GPU support for machine learning?
faq.answer: No. This is a CPU-only environment. Use dedicated AI apps for GPU-accelerated ML workloads.

faq.question: What is the maximum execution time per script?
faq.answer: Up to 300 seconds. The default timeout is 30 seconds, which you can adjust per execution.
